course: How to be a Successful Engineer
speaker: Alex Slocum (guest lecture)
speaker_note: This lecture is delivered by Prof. Alex Slocum (MIT MechE), not by Tom Eagar. Eagar is the course instructor for the term; Slocum is referenced in the third person at p57 ('Professor Eagar, I'm sure, told you...'). Conventions calibrated on Eagar's voice have been applied with caution; Slocum's delivery is more theatrical and digressive than Eagar's. Flagged for editor review.
topics: APHRO (Advanced Pumped Hydro Reverse Osmosis) system
engineering philosophy: catalog-itis, listening for complaints, reciprocity
cases_mentioned: Teradyne semiconductor tester Handler / kinematic coupling solution
APHRO system worldwide siting (Southern California, Mexico, Chile, Peru, China, Iran, UAE)
Wind turbine pole cost reduction (spiral-welded tapered tube)
named_persons: James Clerk Maxwell (referenced as 'Jamie Clerk' — speaker slip)
Mark Graham (former Slocum student, APHRO rap collaborator)
Steven Chu (Secretary of Energy, Deepwater Horizon team)
Truong (Slocum graduate student, kinematic coupling doctoral thesis)
editor_flags: Speaker is Slocum, not Eagar — conventions may need adjustment for Layer 3.
